---
name: Open research direction
about: Propose a new open question the project should track as a research direction
title: "[open-direction] <short question>"
labels: ["type:open-direction", "status:open"]
---

<!--
An open research direction is a well-posed question the papers touch on but
do not settle. It is not a claim to be validated (those use the Validation
template) and not an error to be fixed (those use the Correction template).
It is a question whose resolution would meaningfully extend the project.
See CONTRIBUTING.md § 4 for acceptance criteria.
-->

## The question

<!-- One paragraph, specific enough that progress is conceivable. -->

## Where the papers touch on it

<!-- Quote or cite the relevant paper sections. -->

## What a resolution would look like

<!--
Example:
- "A proof that [statement]."
- "A counterexample showing [statement] is false."
- "An alternative framing that [predicate]."
- "A pointer to prior work that already settles this."
-->

## Why it matters

<!-- How does resolving this change the project? What becomes possible? -->

## Adjacent literature (if you know any)

<!-- Optional. Pointers to papers, preprints, or books that bear on the
question. -->

## Proposer

<!-- Your name, if you'd like to be credited with proposing the direction. -->
